emails.subject: Re: rumor
emails.body: Re: rumor I spent the last few days with Rod at a CFEE conference, and he is definitely going to run..he doesn't think that the election will occur until June so we will at least have him in Sacto until then. The CFEE conference was the best that I have ever attended, there were 10 members of the legislature there, and though the focus was on air quality and power plants, the members were expressing their frustation with the administration in not looking for solutions and only pointing fingers..besides contracts and expidited siting, a large part of the discussion was on educating the public...Senators Kuehl and Machado are planning on putting together a public service announcement of some sort for members to use in their districts on how to conserve energy..do we have anything that would be helpful? Dion Aaroner who chairs the Assembly Democratic Caucus, and Rod will be doing a briefing for the members on energy in January, do we have any basic information, like a glossary of energy terms that I could give her, I will be with her next Thursday. Dion is also talking to the speaker today, briefing him on what the govenor has been doing, and asking him to call the govenor to talk about changing his tactics.

emails.subject: Re: rumor
emails.body: I'm checking w/ EES to see if they have any generic brochures, etc. on energy conservation or a glossary of terms. From: Sandra McCubbin on 12/13/2000 10:15 AM PST
